query:
INSERT INTO public.questions (
    id, question_text, question_type, options, correct_answer, explanation, hint,
    stimulus_text, stimulus_type, section, topic, difficulty, exam_type,
    is_platform_question, ai_generated, calculator_allowed, subject, subtopic,
    math_domain, pool_key, diagnostic_eligible, tags, external_id, source, source_metadata
  ) VALUES
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The sentence "The cat sat on the mat, it was sleeping." is incorrect because it uses a comma splice.  Which of the following revisions fixes the comma splice error?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The cat sat on the mat; it was sleeping.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The cat sat on the mat. It was sleeping.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The cat sat on the mat and it was sleeping.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The cat, sat on the mat; it was sleeping.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'A comma splice occurs when two independent clauses are joined by a comma without a coordinating conjunction or a semicolon.  The sentence is correctly revised by separating the independent clauses with a period.  The other choices are incorrect because they still contain a comma splice, or they use punctuation that doesn’t make sense in the context.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    NULL,
    NULL,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'opensat_14f234c4322eb402_english_01325',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_a9","opensat_index":1325}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In "The Time Traveler’s Wife," the author’s decision to tell the story from Clare’s point of view most likely allows the reader to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"understand the emotional journey of a person who loves someone with a unique condition.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"experience the physical and emotional challenges of time travel.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"explore the complexities of a relationship between a human and a time traveler.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"understand the challenges of navigating a relationship with someone with a mental illness.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The novel focuses on Clare''s experience of loving someone with a unique condition. Niffenegger''s use of Clare''s point of view allows the reader to understand the emotional journey of a person who loves someone with a unique condition.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'In "The Time Traveler''s Wife," author Audrey Niffenegger utilizes a unique narrative structure to portray the story of Clare Abshire and Henry DeTamble. Niffenegger’s choice to depict the story from Clare’s point of view is essential to the novel’s central theme. Clare’s perspective provides insight into the complexity of love, loss, and acceptance. What is the most likely reason why Niffenegger chose to tell the story from Clare’s point of view?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    '541ab21f_english_01326',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"541ab21f","opensat_index":1326}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best combines the sentences at the underlined portion?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The wall is so long that it is made of stone, brick, earth, and wood, and it can be seen from space.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The wall is so long, it is made of stone, brick, earth, and wood, and it can be seen from space.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The wall, being so long, is made of stone, brick, earth, and wood, and it can be seen from space.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The wall is so long, made of stone, brick, earth, and wood, and it can be seen from space.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'Choice D best combines the sentences because it uses a comma to separate the introductory phrase "so long, made of stone, brick, earth, and wood" from the rest of the sentence. This creates a grammatically correct and clear sentence.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications that were built over centuries, and it is one of the most recognizable structures in the world. The wall is made of stone, brick, earth, and wood, and it is so long that it can be seen from space.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '7c2727f0_english_01327',

    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"7c2727f0","opensat_index":1327}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The passage suggests that the most common mistake made by people who are learning to write is to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"use too many commas.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"write too many long sentences.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"use too many complex words.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"use too many short sentences.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The passage states that the most common mistake made by people who are learning to write is to use too many commas. This is supported by the fact that the passage explains that people are taught to use commas in so many different situations, and it can be hard to remember when to use them and when not to use them. Therefore, the most likely reason why people make this mistake is because they use too many commas.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The most common mistake made by people who are learning to write is to use too many commas. In fact, according to a recent study, 90% of high school students make at least one comma error when writing a basic essay. This is probably because people are taught to use commas in so many different situations, and it can be hard to remember when to use them and when not to use them. But while it is true that too many commas can make writing seem choppy, it is also true that a comma in the right place can make writing more clear and concise. Which is a way to improve writing?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'opensat_820c4cabf097905d_english_01328',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_e6","opensat_index":1328}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author’s argument about the new invention is best summarized by which of the following claims?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The new invention is a revolutionary invention that uses the same principles that have been used for centuries to create other types of technology.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The new invention is particularly useful in a variety of fields, such as medicine, engineering, and agriculture.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The new invention is revolutionary because it has the potential to change the world as we know it.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The new invention has been in development for decades, but it has only recently become available to the public.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The author''s main point is that the new invention is revolutionary because it has the potential to change the world as we know it. The author supports this claim by noting that the invention uses well-established principles, has been under development for many years, and has a wide range of applications.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author suggests that the new invention is revolutionary because it uses the same principles that have been used for centuries to create other types of technology. The author also notes that the new invention has been in development for decades and that it has only recently become available to the public. In addition, the author points out that the new invention is particularly useful in a variety of fields, such as medicine, engineering, and agriculture. The author concludes that the new invention has the potential to change the world as we know it. Which of the following is the best summary of the author’s argument about the invention?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    '9317a986_english_01329',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"9317a986","opensat_index":1329}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In this sentence, the author uses commas to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"separate the independent clauses from the dependent clauses.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"separate the introductory phrase from the main clause.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"separate the two independent clauses from each other.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"separate the two dependent clauses from each other.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The sentence contains two complete sentences, "I am afraid so." and "it’s a very painful parting." Commas are used to separate two complete sentences when they are joined by a coordinating conjunction, such as "so." In this case, the author used a comma and the coordinating conjunction "so" to separate the two independent clauses.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author''s use of commas in the sentence is correct because the sentence contains multiple independent clauses. The first clause, "I am afraid so." is a complete sentence, and the second clause, "it’s a very painful parting." is also a complete sentence. The two clauses are joined by a comma and a coordinating conjunction ("so"). Therefore, the author’s use of commas is correct. What is the most likely reason why the author used commas in this sentence?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'd220e27e_english_01330',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"d220e27e","opensat_index":1330}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The passage suggests that filmmakers use a blended approach because they want to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"balance realism with cost.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"avoid using computer-generated imagery whenever possible.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"make the audience aware of the technical aspects of filmmaking.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"create a sense of intimacy between the audience and the film.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The passage states that practical effects are more realistic but more expensive.  Thus, a blended approach is used to balance realism with cost.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author suggests that some artists who want to create more realistic visual effects use a blended approach, employing practical effects when possible and CGI elements when necessary to control costs. The author’s description of the approach is consistent with the idea that practical effects are often more expensive than CGI. According to the passage, what is the main reason why filmmakers use a blended approach?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '9481235b_english_01331',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"9481235b","opensat_index":1331}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice most effectively combines the sentences at the underlined portion?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The telephone allowed people to communicate with each other over long distances, that was previously impossible.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The telephone allowed people to communicate with each other over long distances; which was previously impossible.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The telephone allowed people to communicate with each other over long distances, previously impossible.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The telephone allowed people to communicate with each other over long distances, a thing that was previously impossible.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The most concise and grammatically correct way to combine the sentences is to use a comma and remove the unnecessary word "which."  The original sentence uses a comma splice, which is a grammatical error.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The invention of the telephone was a major breakthrough in communication technology, and it had a profound impact on society.  The telephone allowed people to communicate with each other over long distances, which was previously impossible.  It also helped to make the world a smaller place.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'opensat_8cc39852812143c5_english_01332',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_c8","opensat_index":1332}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which of the following best corrects a grammatical error in the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"While these shows can be entertaining the text states that they also provide \"a rich tapestry of information about the challenges facing our planet.\"","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"While these shows can be entertaining, the text states that they also provide a rich tapestry of information about the challenges facing our planet.\"","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"While these shows can be entertaining, the text states that they also provide \"a rich tapestry\" of information about the challenges facing our planet.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"While these shows can be entertaining the text states that, they also provide \"a rich tapestry of information about the challenges facing our planet.\"","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The original sentence has a comma splice. To correct the error, we need to either separate the independent clauses with a semicolon or add a coordinating conjunction. Only choice B does this correctly.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The popularity of television shows about the natural world has helped to foster a renewed appreciation for the beauty and importance of the planet. While these shows can be entertaining, the text states that they also provide "a rich tapestry of information about the challenges facing our planet."',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '43efb000_english_01333',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"43efb000","opensat_index":1333}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author''s use of literary devices in this passage primarily serves to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"highlight the speaker''s personal journey and experiences.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"create a sense of mystery and suspense.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"convey the beauty and wonder of the natural world.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"persuade the reader to take action.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The author''s use of imagery, personification, and metaphor creates a vivid and engaging description of the natural world, making the passage more immersive and appealing to the reader.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author uses several literary devices in the passage, including imagery, personification, and metaphor. The author uses imagery to create vivid and evocative descriptions of the natural world. For example, the author describes the "sun-drenched hills" and the "crystal-clear stream." The author also uses personification to give human qualities to inanimate objects. For example, the author describes the "wind whispering through the trees" and the "river singing its song." Finally, the author uses metaphor to create comparisons between different things. For example, the author compares the "journey of life" to a "winding road." How does the author''s use of these devices contribute to the overall effect of the passage?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    'opensat_a77647615e5b9f81_english_01334',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_a8","opensat_index":1334}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which of the following statements best summarizes the main idea of the passage? ',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The best way to understand history is to rely on primary sources.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"It is important to consider multiple perspectives and potential biases when examining historical evidence.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Historical events are often complex and difficult to understand.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The study of history is a subjective and ever-evolving process.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The passage highlights the importance of acknowledging multiple perspectives and potential biases when studying history. The author stresses the need for a comprehensive approach that considers all available evidence and viewpoints.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The passage discusses the importance of using a variety of methods and sources to understand the origins of a certain historical event. The author also emphasizes the need to consider multiple perspectives and potential biases when examining historical evidence. Which of the following statements best summarizes the main idea of the passage?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'opensat_afbf9a164f4685de_english_01335',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_eng","opensat_index":1335}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The shift in focus in the passage is most likely intended to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"provide a detailed account of the woman’s personality.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"create a contrast between the woman’s outward appearance and her inner thoughts.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"highlight the importance of the woman’s dreams and aspirations.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"suggest that the woman’s work is not as fulfilling as she would like.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The shift in focus from the woman’s external actions to her internal thoughts creates a contrast between her outward appearance and her inner thoughts. This contrast helps to highlight the complex nature of the woman’s experience and to create a sense of tension and mystery.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The passage opens with a description of a woman working at her desk. The passage then shifts to focus on the woman’s internal thoughts, her anxieties, and her hopes for the future. What is the most likely reason for this shift in focus?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    'opensat_cc2aef2644234ba8_english_01336',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_c4","opensat_index":1336}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best describes how the underlined portion functions in the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"It provides a summary of the speaker’s childhood.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"It introduces the main idea of the passage.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"It contrasts the speaker’s past with the speaker’s present.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"It provides a specific example to support the speaker’s claim.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The underlined portion provides a specific example of a happy childhood memory, which supports the speaker''s earlier claim that it was a happy life. This supports the idea that the main purpose of the passage is to evoke a feeling of nostalgia.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'I thought about the way we used to spend our summers. We used to go to the beach every day, and we would build sandcastles, swim in the ocean, and collect seashells. It was a simple life, but it was a happy one.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    'opensat_af2ecff0877ecb67_english_01337',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_c6","opensat_index":1337}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In the sentence, "I ask that you refrain from looking at your phones during my lecture," the phrase “refrain from” is used to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"imply that the reader is not allowed to use their phone during the lecture.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"emphasize the speaker’s request that the reader avoid using their phone.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"suggest that the reader might be tempted to use their phone during the lecture.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"warn the reader that they will be punished if they use their phone during the lecture.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The phrase "refrain from" suggests that the speaker believes that the reader will be tempted to look at their phone during the lecture.  The writer uses this phrase to emphasize the speaker’s request that the reader avoid using their phone during the lecture.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The use of the phrase “refrain from” suggests that the speaker believes that the reader will be tempted to do something they should avoid, which is to look at their phone during the lecture.  To explain what the speaker believes about the reader, it is most effective to continue by explaining what the reader is likely to be tempted to do during the lecture. What is the most likely reason why the writer uses the phrase “refrain from” in the sentence?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Expression of Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Expression of Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Expression of Ideas']::text[],
    '4c65f78e_english_01338',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Expression of Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"4c65f78e","opensat_index":1338}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"help to","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"helps to","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"helping to","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"helped to","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The sentence requires a finite verb to complete the action of the subject ("eating a healthy diet").  "Help to" provides the finite verb that completes the action of the subject, and it also correctly follows the conventions of Standard English.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'A study by researchers at the University of California, Berkeley, found that people who ate a diet rich in fruits and vegetables were more likely to be healthy.  The study’s findings suggest that eating a healthy diet can ______ improve a person’s overall well-being.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,

    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In this passage, the phrase "pride in his culture’s traditions" is used to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"show that Okonkwo is a strong leader.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"foreshadow that Okonkwo will be threatened by the arrival of the Europeans.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"suggest that Okonkwo will be resistant to change.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"emphasize the importance of tradition in Okonkwo’s culture.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The phrase "pride in his culture’s traditions" suggests that traditions are highly important to Okonkwo and his culture, which makes the upcoming change caused by the Europeans seem more significant.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The passage is about the impact of the arrival of European missionaries in the fictional Nigerian village of Umuofia.  The passage emphasizes that Okonkwo, a village leader, is proud of his traditions and the strength of his culture.  The text does not mention a conflict between the Europeans and Okonkwo, nor does it state that the Europeans have been welcomed by Okonkwo.  What is the most likely reason why the writer uses the phrase "pride in his culture’s traditions" in this excerpt?',

  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which of the following best describes the overall structure of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The passage provides a definition of the word “cool” and then gives examples of its various uses.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The passage discusses the evolution of the word “cool” and then explores its current meanings.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The passage explains the history of the word “cool” and then discusses its social and cultural significance.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The passage argues that the word “cool” has a complex history, but its use has been simplified over time.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The passage is structured as a discussion of the history and social and cultural significance of the word “cool.” The passage begins with a brief overview of the word’s origins, then explores its various shades of meaning, and finally emphasizes the power of the word to reflect the values and attitudes of a particular group or generation. ',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The passage discusses the history of the use of the word “cool” as a slang term. According to the passage, the word “cool” evolved from a literal description of temperature to a more figurative meaning related to detachment and style. The passage points out that the evolution of “cool” is complex, as it can be difficult to distinguish between the various shades of meaning that the word “cool” now carries. For example, the word “cool” can be used to describe a person who is aloof or indifferent, a person who is stylish or fashionable, or a person who is simply calm or relaxed. However, the passage also suggests that the word “cool” is not just a matter of semantics. It can reflect the values and attitudes of a particular group or generation. For example, the passage notes that the word “cool” was particularly popular among teenagers in the 1950s. It’s clear that the word “cool” is a powerful and versatile tool for expressing a variety of meanings and attitudes.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best summarizes the main idea of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Scientific theories must be testable and falsifiable in order to be considered valid.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The theory of relativity is a testable and falsifiable scientific theory that has not been proven false.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Philosophical theories are not testable or falsifiable, but they can still be valuable.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Scientific theories are different from philosophical theories in that they are testable and falsifiable.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The passage emphasizes the importance of testability and falsifiability for scientific theories. It explains that a theory must be able to be tested and potentially proven false through observation or experimentation in order to be considered a valid scientific theory.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The most important thing to consider about scientific theories is whether they are testable and falsifiable. A theory is testable if it can be tested through experimentation or observation. A theory is falsifiable if it can be proven false through experimentation or observation.  If a theory is not testable or falsifiable, then it is not a scientific theory. However, this does not mean that a theory is wrong or useless. For example, the theory of relativity is testable and falsifiable, but it has not been proven false. It is also not testable or falsifiable, but it is also not a scientific theory. Instead, it is a philosophical theory.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',

  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author uses the word "including" in this sentence to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"provide a detailed and specific list of the artist''s accomplishments.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"emphasize the importance of the artist''s accomplishments.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"indicate that the artist''s accomplishments are not limited to the examples given.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"suggest that the artist’s accomplishments are not as impressive as they could be.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The use of "including" implies that the artist''s accomplishments extend beyond those specifically mentioned in the sentence.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The artist''s work has been featured in prestigious exhibitions around the world, including a solo show at the Museum of Modern Art in New York City, and numerous publications, including the New York Times.  What is the most likely reason why the author chose the word ''including'' in this sentence?',

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The structure of the passage can best be described as',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"an explanation of a phenomenon followed by a description of its causes.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"a comparison of two different perspectives on a topic followed by a conclusion.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"a presentation of a controversial claim followed by a counterargument.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"a discussion of the benefits and drawbacks of a practice.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The passage begins by presenting the phenomenon that procrastination is linked to creativity, then it explains why that might be the case. This makes the structure of the passage an explanation of a phenomenon followed by a description of its causes.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'A new study by researchers at the University of California, Berkeley, explores the relationship between creativity and procrastination. The study found that people who procrastinate are more likely to come up with new and innovative ideas. This finding is explained by the researchers’ argument that, although procrastination may seem like a negative habit, it can actually be a source of inspiration and creativity. The researchers suggest that when people procrastinate, they are not simply avoiding work but are instead allowing their minds to wander and explore new possibilities. This mental exploration can lead to unexpected breakthroughs and innovative solutions. While some may view procrastination as a sign of laziness or disorganization, the study suggests that it can be a valuable tool for generating creative ideas.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The sentence is most likely intended to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"provide a brief overview of the car’s design before describing the car’s production.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"contrast the car’s design with the car’s production process.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"provide a detailed explanation of the car’s cutting-edge technology.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"explain how the car’s design has revolutionized the automotive industry.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The sentence introduces the car’s design and then provides a general overview of the design before the passage goes on to describe the car’s production. This is an example of how a passage can be structured to give a clear and concise explanation of a topic.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The passage describes the process of designing and building a new kind of car. The writer begins by describing the car’s design: its exterior is sleek, its interior is spacious, and its technology is cutting-edge. Then the writer describes the car’s production: it is built by hand, and the materials used are of the highest quality. Finally, the writer reflects on the car’s impact on the automotive industry: it has revolutionized the way people view and experience car design.  In the passage, what is the purpose of the sentence:  "The writer begins by describing the car’s design: its exterior is sleek, its interior is spacious, and its technology is cutting-edge." ?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which of the following is the most effective way to combine the sentences in this passage so that they conform to the conventions of Standard English?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The discovery of a new species of deep-sea fish, the *Pseudoliparis swirei*,  is significant because it was found in the deepest known part of the Mariana Trench, the *P. swirei* is small, translucent, and lacks scales, and it is a reminder of the vastness of the deep ocean.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The discovery of a new species of deep-sea fish, the *Pseudoliparis swirei*,  is significant because it was found in the deepest known part of the Mariana Trench: the *P. swirei* is small, translucent, and lacks scales, and it is a reminder of the vastness of the deep ocean.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The discovery of a new species of deep-sea fish, the *Pseudoliparis swirei*, is significant because it was found in the deepest known part of the Mariana Trench; the *P. swirei* is small, translucent, and lacks scales, and it is a reminder of the vastness of the deep ocean.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The discovery of a new species of deep-sea fish, the *Pseudoliparis swirei*, is significant because it was found in the deepest known part of the Mariana Trench, the *P. swirei* is small, translucent, and lacks scales, and it is a reminder of the vastness of the deep ocean.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The most effective way to combine the sentences is to use a comma to separate the two independent clauses.  The second clause is nonessential, and the comma helps to clarify the relationship between the two clauses.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The discovery of a new species of deep-sea fish, the *Pseudoliparis swirei*,  is significant because it was found in the deepest known part of the Mariana Trench.  The *P. swirei* is small, translucent, and lacks scales, and it is a reminder of the vastness of the deep ocean.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,

  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The Belle Époque was a period of relative peace and prosperity in France, marked by technological advancements, economic growth, and a flourishing of the arts.',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The Belle Époque era was a period of relative peace and prosperity in France, marked by technological advancements, economic growth, and a flourishing of the arts.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The Belle Époque, a period of relative peace and prosperity in France, was marked by technological advancements, economic growth, and a flourishing of the arts.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The Belle Époque era, a period of relative peace and prosperity in France, was marked by technological advancements, economic growth, and a flourishing of the arts.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The Belle Époque, a period of relative peace and prosperity in France, marked by technological advancements, economic growth, and a flourishing of the arts.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The sentence is a nonrestrictive appositive, which means it adds extra information about the Belle Époque. Nonrestrictive appositives are set off with commas. Choice B is the only choice that correctly uses commas to set off the appositive. Choices A and C are incorrect because they do not use commas to set off the appositive. Choice D is incorrect because it uses a comma after the appositive, which is not necessary.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The world’s largest collection of Impressionist paintings, housed in the Musée d’Orsay in Paris, was formerly a railway station. Constructed in the 1900s, the station was designed to be an elegant and modern gateway to the city.  However, it was abandoned by 1939, and the building was later repurposed to house the museum’s vast collection of art. Because of its unique history, the Musée d’Orsay is often called a testament to both the beauty and the ephemerality of the Belle Époque era.  The Belle Époque was a period of relative peace and prosperity in France, marked by technological advancements, economic growth, and a flourishing of the arts. Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,

    false,

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice provides the best revision of the underlined portion of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"was credited with creating the first working prototype in 1964.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"is credited with creating the first working prototype in 1964.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"has been credited with creating the first working prototype in 1964.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"was being credited with creating the first working prototype in 1964.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The underlined portion is in the past tense, so the verb should also be in the past tense.  The past tense verb "was credited"  correctly reflects the past tense of the rest of the sentence.  Choice B is incorrect because the verb is in the present tense, which doesn''t match the tense of the rest of the sentence. Choice C is incorrect because the verb "has been credited" is in the present perfect tense.  Choice D is incorrect because the verb "was being credited" is in the past progressive tense.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The history of the development of the computer mouse is long and complex, but it was Douglas Engelbart who is credited with creating the first working prototype in 1964. Engelbart''s invention was a revolutionary concept in the field of human-computer interaction, one that would change the way people interact with technology forever.  His invention was a small wooden box with two metal wheels that could be moved across a flat surface, and it allowed users to control the cursor on a computer screen by rolling the box. The mouse''s invention had a profound impact on the development of computers and the world, and it is still used by millions of people every day.',
    'passage',

    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice provides the best way to combine the sentences in the underlined portion without changing the meaning?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Many people found the new styles to be too experimental and jarring, and they resisted the change in popular music, but psychedelic and folk rock have since been recognized as two of the most influential genres of rock music in the 1960s.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Many people found the new styles to be too experimental and jarring, and they resisted the change in popular music; psychedelic and folk rock have since been recognized as two of the most influential genres of rock music in the 1960s.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Many people found the new styles to be too experimental and jarring, resisting the change in popular music, but psychedelic and folk rock have since been recognized as two of the most influential genres of rock music in the 1960s.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Many people found the new styles to be too experimental and jarring, so they resisted the change in popular music, but psychedelic and folk rock have since been recognized as two of the most influential genres of rock music in the 1960s.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'Choice A is the only choice that correctly combines the two sentences without changing the meaning. The other choices use punctuation that is either incorrect or that changes the meaning of the text.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The 1960s saw the birth of many new genres of popular music.  One of the most notable was psychedelic rock. It’s a genre of rock music that is characterized by the use of electronic effects, distorted guitars, and mind-altering lyrics.  Another genre to emerge was folk rock, which was more acoustic and blended elements of traditional folk music with rock.  These styles of music were not always well-received. Many people found the new styles to be too experimental and jarring, and they resisted the change in popular music. But psychedelic and folk rock have since been recognized as two of the most influential genres of rock music in the 1960s.  They both have continued to influence a wide range of musical artists, from indie rockers to pop stars.',
    'passage',
